meta.title: ProxMenux Monitor — Dashboard | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: The dashboard is the main UI of ProxMenux Monitor: nine tabs (System Overview, Storage, Network, VMs & LXCs, Hardware, System Logs, Terminal, Security, Settings) plus the global header with the Health Monitor status pill.

header.title: Dashboard
header.description: The dashboard is the everyday view of ProxMenux Monitor — nine tabs each focused on one slice of the host plus a global header with the Health Monitor status pill, the node identity and the quick-refresh control.

oneHeader.title: One header, nine tabs
oneHeader.body: The header (logo, node name, status pill, uptime, refresh, theme toggle) stays visible everywhere. The active tab below it changes the entire content area. The status pill colour mirrors the worst category of the <link>Health Monitor</link> — it's the same data point seen from the dashboard.

tabs.heading: The nine tabs
tabs.intro: Each tab has its own dedicated page. Pages are added incrementally as the documentation is filled in; below is the full list and what each one is responsible for.

rows.name: System Overview
rows.owns: CPU / memory / temperature widgets, active VM & LXC count, historical metrics charts, storage and network summaries. Default landing tab.

rows.name: Storage
rows.owns: Proxmox pools, physical disks, SMART data, ZFS state, wear & lifetime, observation history.

rows.name: Network
rows.owns: Every interface (physical / bond / bridge / OVS), IP/MAC, RX/TX graphs, historical RRD per interface.

rows.name: VMs & LXCs
rows.owns: Inventory of guests, drill-in for config / metrics / logs, start / stop / reboot / shutdown actions.

rows.name: Hardware
rows.owns: CPU model and topology, memory layout, PCIe topology, GPUs with per-slot real-time monitoring.

rows.name: System Logs
rows.owns: Live journalctl with filters, Proxmox task history, notification log, downloadable log bundles.

rows.name: Terminal
rows.owns: Browser shell to host or to any VM/CT, powered by xterm.js over WebSockets.

rows.name: Security
rows.owns: Auth setup, password / 2FA / API tokens, audit log, optional Fail2Ban panel, Secure Gateway deployment.

rows.name: Settings
rows.owns: Notification channels, AI provider, suppression durations, branding, advanced flags.

headerAnatomy.heading: Header anatomy
items: Logo + product name on the left. The logo turns into an "update available" variant when a newer Monitor release is detected.
Node identity — the Proxmox node name resolved from pvesh get /nodes, falling back to hostname.

Health status pill — Healthy (green), Warning (yellow), Critical (red). Click it to open the Health Monitor modal. An extra blue info badge appears when there are dismissed items still inside their suppression window.
Refresh button — re-issues all the live API calls without a full page reload.
Theme toggle — light / dark / system. Persisted in localStorage.

items.label: Health Monitor
items.tail: — the modal behind the header status pill, ten categories deep-dive.

items.label: Architecture
items.tail: — how the dashboard talks to the Flask backend.
